sections.paragraph:
στάσις, εως, ἡ [ἵστημι]

—1. ‘existence in a place’, standing ἔχειν στάσιν be in place Hb 9:8. In transferred sense

—2. ‘a position or stance that challenges public order’, uprising, sedition, insurgency Lk 23:19, 25; Ac 19:40. Of a specific well-known uprising ἐν τῇ στάσει Mk 15:7. In related sense but with less volatile connotation

—3. ‘a circumstance characterized by counter-positioning’, dissension, discord Ac 15:2; 23:7, 10; 24:5.
